reasoning:
The United States was consistently in the top tiers but has shown a sharp decline over the past decade. By the 2024 Academic Freedom Index update (reflecting 2023 data), news reports indicated there were 87 countries above the US in academic freedom out of 179 total, placing it at exactly 88th. Since 89.5 is the median for 179 countries, the US was precariously perched at the very bottom edge of the Top 50% (in the Top 40-50% bracket). 

The question asks whether the US will be in the Bottom 50% for the year 2025 (typically evaluated in the 2026 report). Given the intense political pressure on higher education in 2024 — including the fallout from anti-war campus protests, congressional hearings that led to the resignation of university presidents, crackdowns on student demonstrations, and continuing state-level bans on DEI and certain academic concepts — the subjective assessments by AFI's expert coders will almost certainly reflect a further decline. Furthermore, a second Trump term starting in 2025 brings stated policy goals of significantly interfering with university accreditation, diversity programs, and funding. Given this environment, the US academic freedom score is highly likely to continue its documented downward slide. 

Because the US only needs to drop two spots relative to other countries to fall into the bottom 50%, and considering the uniquely intense spotlight and political attacks on American academia over 2024-2025, I estimate a 75% probability that the US will be ranked in the bottom 50% for 2025.
